pubmed_links: [Moon et al. (2024) Knockout of intellectual disability-linked Hs6st2 decreases 6-O-sulfation, impairs dendritic spines and memory](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38015989/)
summary: HS6ST3 encodes one of three heparan sulfate 6-O-sulfotransferases expressed in the brain; while HS6ST3 itself lacks direct published disease associations, closely related family members HS6ST1 and HS6ST2 are linked to brain malformations and intellectual disability, and heparan sulfate deficiency broadly has been implicated in autism spectrum disorder pathology. (novel candidate)
